And then as we started to scale, and we were using like low-code tools like Aerofs and NAN, what started happening is like there was a lot of manual work.

And so as we grow, it's becoming extremely harder and harder to give people the whole context.
And so, and we need to have really consistent outputs.
And so sometimes even the way somebody is prompting or giving some instructions in our workflows will have a big impact in the output.

Get, you know, 100 pages live, like edit, stage things, and just like post a stand up.

But there's also value in the strategy because if you don't get the strategy right, like it doesn't matter the workflows or anything you're doing.

And so the whole mechanic of the business
This was like, essentially like Kavishka plus strategy plus the workflow as a service.
And I knew that like, just, you know, somebody like Kavishka alone, companies would be willing to pay, you know, at me as a CMO, like $9,000 a month, right?
